response.justification: Szabo is a talented senior engineer with strong distributed systems and architectural expertise, but the role fundamentally requires current C++ mastery and industrial automation domain knowledge. His C++ experience ended in 2015 (10+ years ago) and represents only 6 months of his career; the past decade has focused on web/cloud/backend development. Additionally, he lacks any mention of industrial cybersecurity, SDL practices, IEC 62443 standards, MQTT protocols, or IoT/embedded systems experience—all core to this position. While his seniority level matches, the technical and domain gaps are too large to bridge.
strengths: 11+ years as Senior/Staff engineer with proven architectural thinking and distributed systems expertise (microservices, event-driven platforms)
Strong mentorship and knowledge-sharing track record (code dojos, workshops, open-source contributions, staff roles)
Relevant advantageous skills: extensive Node.js, React, and TypeScript expertise; some IoT/GIS platform background
concerns: C++ expertise is stale (last significant work Dec 2014–Jun 2015, 10+ years ago) and has not been primary focus in recent roles
No demonstrated background in industrial automation, embedded systems, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, IEC 61850), or secure development lifecycle practices
Career has consistently focused on web/cloud/backend development rather than systems programming or industrial/real-time software domains

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (stale, not primary skill)
Industrial automation and embedded systems domain gap
